rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENA MAE RENFRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/27/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (see tentative rulings for past hearings): 1) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of Esma Renfro (who survived the decedent and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 2) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of James Renfro (who survived the decedent and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 3) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of Belinda Renfro (who survived the decedent and James Renfro and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. Note: Belinda Renfro's successors must sign two affidavits: one for the estate of James Renfro; and one for the estate of Belinda Renfro. See Probate Code sections 11801(a), 11802(c), and 13100 et seq; see also LRSF 14.35(G) & 14.59(C)(2)(6). If a small estate affidavit cannot be obtained from all the successors (or cannot be used for any other reason), distribution must be made under section 11802(a). A certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative within 60 days before the hearing must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (CANNOT be e-filed), or a certification of authority for summary administration issued by a public administrator under section 7660(a)(2)(A) must be filed if distribution is made to the public administrator acting as personal representative to proceed with summary disposition. Note: An appearance may be required on 03/27/2024 if no papers are filed to show satisfactory progress to address the issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORRAINE D. ELLIOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Pay The Fee Of The Administrators; Pay The Ordinary Attorneys' Fees And Costs; Distribute The Balance Of The Estate To The Heirs Of The Decedent And Discharge The Petitioner
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/11/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of the second amended petition has been filed. As previously noted, the ONLY CORRECT FORM OF NOTICE is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing. (2) Notice must include the instructions regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) There is a reference to approval of a settlement agreement in this second amended petition, but the pleading caption does not reflect this. [CRC 7.102.] (4) The estate was appraised at $835,000. Statutory compensation is awarded by the Court (5) The prayer is entirely inadequate: the amount of fees and costs is not set forth; the specific proposed distribution is not set forth; and there is no legal description for the real property. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (6) The verification refers to the amended petition, not this second amended petition. (7) Under what authority is Stephanie Dobbs serving as the sole successor trustee of Raymond Elliott's trust? Her declaration, filed on 3/3/23, showed that Raymond Elliott's trust named both Stephanie Dobbs and Craig Yancey Elliott as successor trustee or co-trustees. Did Craig decline? (8) Why are assets from this estate to be distributed to Raymond Elliott's heirs, not to the trustee(s) of his trust? (9) If Jennifer Rezentes is the personal representative of Kenneth Elliott's estate, distribution should be made to her in that capacity, not to Kenneth Elliott's heirs. Without proof of her court-appointment as such personal representative, it is unclear that she had authority to enter into a settlement agreement. (10) No revised proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/29/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIGUEL Q URIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/13/24. (No earlier availability.) 1. Notice must include the procedures for making a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] 2. Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, not in care of another. If a party entitled to notice is represented by an attorney, notice must also be given to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b)]. 3. When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. 4. Since no I&A has been filed yet, if the Court were to proceed with consideration of this accounting, original bank statements or some verification of the assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period should also be filed. 5. Attorney costs paid during administration must be itemized. 6. Did the parties who received electronic service consent to electronic service? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNIE LUNG LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Neither the mailed nor published notice indicated that the petitioner seeks to have a WILL admitted. (2) Furthermore, the proof of service filed on 1/10/24 was incomplete at item 1. (3) Chi Wah Lung was not, in fact, deceased when the decedent died; he post-deceased in 2022. (4) If Chi Wah Lung and Joseph Lung were or are the successor trustee(s) of the devisee trust, and Chi Wah Lung is now deceased and Joseph Lung declines to serve as successor trustee, there is no one with any standing to represent the devisee trust. (5) None of the waivers of bond indicates in item 4 who the proposed personal representative is. (6) Item 9 of the petition is erroneous, as there are several pages attached to the petition. (7) Does the petitioner not know for certain whether or not Decedent was remarried to Paul Lau? Deadline is 3/1/24 for providing courtesy copies to the examiner of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TERESITA H. RAVETTI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jeffrey M. Hall Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 430.41
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Appearance required. Counsel for the parties are ordered to be prepared to discuss the following: 1. Proper venue for matters concerning Mrs. Ravetti's personal directives and care (see Cal. Prob. Code section 4763.) 2. Is either court's (San Francisco Superior and San Diego Superior) legal ability or authority to hear a matter concerning Mrs. Ravetti's AHCD limited by the "order of priority" for the "proper county for commencement of a proceeding under this division" listed in Prob. Code section 4763? 3. The enforceability of Mrs. Ravetti's 2015 Advanced Health Care Directive ("AHCD") during pendency of both First Amended Petition, etc. ("FAP") in San Diego County, and Petition to Enforce Agent's Powers Under AHCD, etc. in this court. 4. The parties filed 2/28/23 Joint Stipulation to Change Venue and Transfer Action to San Diego County Superior Court, agreeing to transfer "the action" of PTR-22-305380 to San Diego Superior Court. The First Amended Petition, as filed in this court 9/2/22, was thus transferred to San Diego County Superior Court. Notwithstanding the issue of proper venue for matters concerning Mrs. Ravetti personally, or the Opposition's argument that the First Amended Petition was invalidly filed while Petition to Transfer was pending, what is the legal impact of this joint stipulation to transfer the entire action of the First Amended Petition to San Diego Superior Court, on this court's jurisdiction over issues raised in the First Amended Petition and the Petition to Enforce Agent's Powers, etc. - specifically the validity and enforceability of the 2015 AHCD and Omstead's appointment as Mrs. Ravetti's agent? To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTIN SEYMOUR MORDKOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner filed the decedent's 2021 will and initially sought admission of the will to probate and his appointment as executor named in the will. However, the petitioner withdrew his petition for probate because he could not locate the two witnesses who signed the will. The petitioner has since filed a new petition for letters of administration to request the Court's finding that the decedent died intestate. Probate Code section 8221 provides that: "If no subscribing witness is available as a witness within the meaning of Section 240 of the Evidence Code, the court may, if the will on its face conforms to all requirements of law, permit proof of the will by proof of the handwriting of the testator and one of the following: (a) Proof of the handwriting of any one subscribing witness; or (b) Receipt in evidence of one of the following documents reciting facts showing due execution of the will: (1) A writing in the will bearing the signatures of all subscribing witnesses; or (2) An affidavit of a person with personal knowledge of the circumstances of the execution. 2) Notice of this petition has not been given to Mark Ravitz and Rose Fong, specific devisees named in the deceden't will, although notice is not required by section 8110. It is unclear whether they received notice of the prior petition to probate the will because no proof of service was filed before that petition was withdrawn. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LARRY DEAN COATE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/11/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to former counsel on 10/27/2023 - see also tentative rulings for 11/27/2023 & 01/03/2024 hearings): 1) What day of February 2017 did the petitioner see the decedent sign and acknowledge the will? 2) Jennifer Walters' complete zip code must be listed at item 8. 3) Arithinat Surawatthana's address listed at item 8 must match the address listed (in English) on the proof of service attached to the DE-121 form. 4) Attachment 2 must describe anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate, even if the total amount of such liabilities and claims does not exceed $20,000. 5) If the petitioner is one of the only two witnesses to the will, allegations must be made to rebut the presumption under section 6112(c) that the petitioner procured her devise by duress, menace, fraud, or undue influence, as the issue may affect the petitioner's eligibility for appointment under sections 8402(a)(3) & 8502. Note: An appearance may be required on 03/11/2024, if papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are not filed.
